description:
The shosts.equiv file lists remote hosts and users that are trusted by the local system. To remove these files, run the following command to delete them from any location: 
$ sudo rm /[path]/[to]/[file]/shosts.equiv
rationale:
The shosts.equiv files are used to configure host-based authentication for the system via SSH.
Host-based authentication is not sufficient for preventing unauthorized access to the system,
as it does not require interactive identification and authentication of a connection request,
or for the use of two-factor authentication.
ocil:
Verify that there are no shosts.equiv files on the system, run the following command: 
$ find / -name shosts.equiv
fixtext:
Remove any found "shosts.equiv" files from the system.

$ sudo rm  /[path]/[to]/[file]/shosts.equiv
srg_requirement: There must be no shosts.equiv files on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9

stig.srg_requirement: There must be no shosts.equiv files on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.
stig.vuldiscussion: The shosts.equiv files are used to configure host-based authentication for the system via SSH. Host-based authentication is not sufficient for preventing unauthorized access to the system, as it does not require interactive identification and authentication of a connection request, or for the use of two-factor authentication.
stig.checktext:
Verify there are no "shosts.equiv" files on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 with the following command:

$ sudo find / -name shosts.equiv

If a "shosts.equiv" file is found, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Remove any found "shosts.equiv" files from the system.

$ sudo rm /[path]/[to]/[file]/shosts.equiv

title: Remove Host-Based Authentication Files
